prose:
**Fukui Reactivity Analysis of Benzaldehyde** using DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fit) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0 (neutral), multiplicity 1. The Fukui condensed indices were computed from Mulliken atomic charges evaluated at the neutral state and charged N+1 (cation, doublet) and N−1 (anion, doublet) states. All three electronic states converged successfully. The charge conservation checks (sum f⁺ = 1.000, sum f⁻ = 1.000) confirm convergence of the N±1 states. 

**Most electrophilic site (highest f⁺):** Atom 3, carbon (ortho to aldehyde), with f⁺ = 0.399 and dual descriptor Δf = 0.363.

**Most nucleophilic site (highest f⁻):** Atom 1, oxygen (aldehyde), with f⁻ = 0.238 and high f⁺ = 0.379 indicating ambiphilicity.

The complete atom-condensed Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, Δf) are recorded above in tabular form. The dual descriptor Δf = f⁺ − f⁻ characterizes electrophilicity (Δf > 0) vs. nucleophilicity (Δf < 0) at each site. Visualization plot: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/argo_claude-haiku-4.5/20260702-195611_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`. Integrity status: trustworthy.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
